summary:
Anticoagulation is strictly contraindicated after intracerebral hemorrhage due to a significant increase in risks of rebleeding and hematoma expansion; all major guidelines recommend immediate discontinuation of anticoagulants and administration of reversal therapies. There is no supportive evidence allowing continued or restarted anticoagulation in the presence of existing hemorrhage.  

(Note: This summary adheres exactly to two concise sentences focusing solely on clinically critical facts.)
